The Ugly

- No manual save system is gross.
- The balancing of moral, resources, % chance, research cost, item build cost....its all over the place. Sometimes its easy and you fly through it, other times it spikes ridiculously.
- The worst offender in the entire game is the game punishes you for exploring. That does not feel fun at all. I get the design choice of stopping players sitting resource farming, but having your crew lose moral isn't fun. I'd rather have an FTL-esque mega ship chasing us out. Like a Borg Cube or something. Later on in the game it could close off certain planets or slow down the journey. But forcing players to stop exploring defeats the point of space exploration games. Especially when it just feels really arbitrary.

Love the tv show. Not this game.  Even set to the easiest setting, you will always be losing.  Trying to balance the amount of issues is more frustrating than fun.   Allow more time to just do other things aside from savaging for raw materials every second.   It ruins the game completely.  You will run out, and everything becomes damaged.   The second you rebuild… it happens instantly again.   Fun but wait to buy on sale maybe.  Even then I’m disappointed with it.  Repetitive and boring.  Add more voice acting maybe.  Needs a big update to me.
Tip for the game developers, invest in text to voice tech, because I am not buying a game to read all throughout the gameplay.

Hello, I'm not talking about games!
And about the injustice to the players from Russia and Belarus!
I live in Russia and I always thought that sports and the gaming industry (video games) are without politics.
As I have seen, none of this is true!
Yes, I understand that all these sanctions are due to the war with Ukraine.
My question is, why weren't players in Europe, the United States, and Japan restricted when the United States and Europe invaded Yugoslavia in 1999, or when the United States and Europe invaded Iraq, why were European and American players quietly playing and buying games and consoles?
Why is this so unfair?
I can't buy a console or a game disc in Russia, but that's okay, I'll find workarounds and buy it.
I wanted to register my Sony PlayStation account, but I couldn't, even choosing another country, I couldn't register.
Of course, this made me angry, of course I knew that your long-time competitor was Nintendo, Xbox is not taken into account, because Xbox practically shut down when it announced that it was moving to the digital world in the likeness of Steam. 
And of course I registered a Nintendo account, naturally choosing another country, but I registered a Nintendo account, I couldn't get a PlayStation account.
3 years ago, I bought a Nintendo Switch Oled console and buy Nintendo cartridges and quietly play Nintendo games.
I have a console, an account, and games on Nintendo cartridges.
And as for PlayStation games, I also play PlayStation games, but for free, using PlayStation and Torrent emulators.
Nintendo has deceived you again, it has taken away players from Russia and Belarus by registering accounts from other countries.
Can't wait!
